title: The Martian
logline: When an astronaut is presumed dead and left behind on Mars during a dust storm evacuation, he must use his ingenuity and scientific knowledge to survive alone on the hostile planet while NASA works to bring him home.
antagonistOrConflict: The harsh environment of Mars and the challenge of survival
tone: Optimistic, humorous, and scientifically grounded despite life-threatening circumstances
themes: The power of science and problem-solving

actBreaks.description: End of setup - Mark is stranded and NASA announces his death
Major turning point - Iris probe explodes, forcing the Hermes crew decision
Final act begins - Launch sequence and rescue attempt

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: The script follows a classic three-act structure but uses parallel storytelling between Mars (Mark's survival) and Earth (NASA's rescue efforts). The middle act is extended to accommodate the long journey and multiple complications.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: Fast-paced opening with the storm and evacuation, then settles into a rhythm of problem-solving montages punctuated by crises. The final act accelerates dramatically with the launch and rescue sequence.

incitingIncident.description: The crew battles fierce winds and zero visibility fighting toward the MAV as Mark suggests using cables to stabilize it, until debris strikes him down

midpoint.description: Bruce energetically addresses his team about building a new Iris probe in 28 days thanks to China's help, having previously completed one in 63 days.

climax.description: Lewis counter-thrusts with her MMU to slow down as Mark approaches at 11 meters distance

scenes.heading: EXT. SPACE - MARS - TO ESTABLISH
scenes.summary: Mars eclipses the Sun as sunlight breaks across the planet's surface, establishing the alien world where the story will unfold

scenes.heading: EXT. MARS - DAY
scenes.summary: The camera moves through Acidalia Planitia to find the Ares 3 habitat site as Commander Lewis radios the team to stay together on Sol 18

scenes.heading: EXT. MARS - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark Watney conducts EVA experiments, chipping rocks and recording observations with bright optimism, describing particle analysis to his crewmates

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Dr. Beck studies samples in the hab while making radio jokes about Martinez's language over the comm system

scenes.heading: EXT. MARS - DAY
scenes.summary: Martinez asks if Beck insulted him while Mark confirms the doctor's wit, with someone offering to cut their radio communications

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Johanssen monitors communications from inside the hab, ready to cut off the banter between crewmates at the commander's word

scenes.heading: EXT. MARS - DAY
scenes.summary: Lewis shuts off Mark and Martinez's radios mid-conversation, apologizing to Vogel for her countrymen's behavior while they work on sample collection

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Johanssen receives an alarming mission update from Houston about the approaching storm, her face going pale at the news

scenes.heading: EXT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: The storm warning escalates as Johanssen reports the upgraded threat level, with the storm visible on the horizon and wind already affecting the surface
scenes.turningPointNote: Storm threat escalates beyond safe parameters

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: The crew learns the storm will generate 8600 Newtons of force, exceeding the MAV's 7500 Newton abort threshold, forcing mission abandonment

scenes.heading: EXT. HAB - STORM - DAY
scenes.summary: The crew battles fierce winds and zero visibility fighting toward the MAV as Mark suggests using cables to stabilize it, until debris strikes him down
scenes.turningPointNote: Mark is struck by antenna debris and disappears

scenes.heading: INT. MAV - CONTINUOUS - DAY
scenes.summary: Martinez reaches the MAV and reports it's tilting at 8 degrees, dangerously close to the 12.3 degree tipping point

scenes.heading: EXT. HAB - STORM - CONTINUOUS - DAY
scenes.summary: Beck receives Mark's final bio-monitor readings showing zero blood pressure and pulse but normal temperature, indicating death to the crew

scenes.heading: INT. MAV - CONTINUOUS - DAY
scenes.summary: The remaining crew members reach the MAV airlock as the situation becomes increasingly desperate

scenes.heading: EXT. STORM - CONTINUOUS - DAY
scenes.summary: Lewis searches blindly in the storm using drill bits to probe the ground, refusing to abandon her crewmate despite the impossible conditions

scenes.heading: INT. MAV - CONTINUOUS - DAY
scenes.summary: The crew discusses detection options while the MAV tilts to 11.6 degrees, with Martinez warning one good gust will tip them over

scenes.heading: EXT. STORM - CONTINUOUS - DAY
scenes.summary: Lewis continues her desperate search through the storm as Martinez reports no contact on proximity radar

scenes.heading: INT. MAV - CONTINUOUS - DAY
scenes.summary: The MAV begins tipping past the point of no return as Martinez prepares his final desperate measure to save the ship

scenes.heading: EXT. MAV - CONTINUOUS - DAY
scenes.summary: Martinez fires the orbital maneuvering thrusters to fight against the MAV's dangerous tilt in a last-ditch stabilization attempt

scenes.heading: INT. MAV - CONTINUOUS - DAY
scenes.summary: The crew urges Lewis to return as they accept Mark's death, with Martinez's thruster burn barely holding the ship stable

scenes.heading: EXT. STORM - CONTINUOUS - DAY
scenes.summary: Lewis stands alone in the storm, hearing confirmation that Watney is gone and facing the impossible decision to abandon him

scenes.heading: INT. MAV - CONTINUOUS - DAY
scenes.summary: Lewis finally accepts the inevitable and returns to the MAV as Martinez maintains precarious control of the tilting vehicle

scenes.heading: INT. MAV - AIRLOCK - CONTINUOUS - DAY
scenes.summary: Lewis enters the MAV and reluctantly gives the launch command, abandoning Mars and her presumed-dead crewmate as the ship lifts off
scenes.turningPointNote: Crew abandons Mars, leaving Mark for dead

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - PRESS ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: NASA Director Teddy Sanders announces the storm emergency and successful crew evacuation to the press, reporting one fatality

scenes.heading: EXT. MARS - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark awakens alone on Mars with a critical injury, the antenna piercing his suit as his oxygen alarm blares in the aftermath
scenes.turningPointNote: Mark survives and awakens alone on Mars

scenes.heading: EXT. MARS - HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark struggles to the hab site and discovers the empty MAV launch pad, confirming his crew has left him behind on the hostile planet

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - AIRLOCK - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark barely makes it to the hab's airlock, tearing off his helmet as soon as the chamber equalizes and he can breathe

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark performs emergency self-surgery to remove the antenna fragment, treating his wound with limited medical supplies in excruciating pain

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark records his first video log explaining his survival and injury, surprising himself that he's still alive on Sol 19

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Mark sits alone in his bunk at night, drumming his fingers and thinking through his impossible situation

scenes.heading: EXT. MARS - DAWN
scenes.summary: Dawn breaks on Sol 21 as Mark continues to assess his dire circumstances on the Red Planet

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark studies the waste processing system and vacuum-sealed waste bags, beginning to formulate ideas for survival

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark inventories the food supplies, including ration packs and a special Thanksgiving meal, calculating his limited resources

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DAWN
scenes.summary: After a sleepless night of planning, Mark begins calculating survival mathematics with pencil and paper, choosing to fight for his life
scenes.turningPointNote: Mark decides to fight for survival

scenes.heading: EXT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark collects the processed waste bags from outside the hab, gathering materials for his agricultural plan

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark mixes water, compost, and human waste despite his revulsion, creating fertilizer for his farming attempt on Sol 24

scenes.heading: EXT. MARS - HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark scoops Martian soil with a small shovel, beginning the laborious process of creating farmable earth

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark continues hauling Martian dirt into the hab on Sol 25, slowly building his growing medium

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: By Sol 28, Mark has accumulated a massive pile of Martian soil inside the hab for his agricultural project

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark spreads the horrific compost mixture over the Martian dirt on Sol 31, enduring the awful smell to create farmable soil

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark plants potato quarters throughout the hab, having converted the entire living space into farmland with methodical precision
scenes.turningPointNote: Mark transforms hab into farm

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark discovers a data stick containing 1970s TV shows while finishing his soil preparation, finding entertainment on Sol 36

scenes.heading: INT./EXT - ROVER - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark carefully extracts hydrazine from the Mars Descent Vehicle, planning to create water by burning the dangerous rocket fuel

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark constructs an improvised chemistry lab with trash bags and air hoses, beginning his dangerous water-making experiments

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark explains the extreme dangers of burning hydrogen while acknowledging NASA's fire-safety policies, preparing for risky chemistry

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark's hydrogen experiment explodes violently, blasting him across the room when he miscalculates the oxygen levels

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: A singed and scorched Mark explains his explosive mistake to the camera, comparing his failure to JPL's founding story

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark successfully creates water by burning hydrazine after correcting his calculations, transforming the hab into a humid rainforest environment

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DAY/NIGHT
scenes.summary: Time-lapse photography shows Mark's daily routine of water production, soil maintenance, and survival activities over many sols

scenes.heading: EXT. SPACE
scenes.summary: The scene transitions from Mars to Earth viewed from space, showing the blue-green planet as a peaceful contrast to the Red Planet

scenes.heading: EXT. ARLINGTON NATIONAL CEMETERY - DAY
scenes.summary: Teddy delivers a eulogy at Mark's memorial service at Arlington National Cemetery, honoring his sacrifice and NASA's commitment to continue their mission.

scenes.heading: EXT. NASA - DAY
scenes.summary: Vincent Kapoor arrives at Johnson Space Center in Houston as the memorial service continues.

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - DAY
scenes.summary: Vincent enters NASA headquarters where security guards are watching news coverage of the presidential memorial service for Mark.

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - VARIOUS - DAY
scenes.summary: Throughout NASA, all employees are watching news reports of the Watney memorial service, including footage of crew members eulogizing Mark.

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - TEDDY’S OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: Vincent meets with Teddy Sanders in his office, requesting satellite authorization to survey remaining Ares 3 supplies on Mars for a potential sixth mission.

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - MISSION CONTROL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Mindy Park processes Vincent's satellite request and discovers something shocking in the overhead images of the Hab site - signs of recent activity.
scenes.turningPointNote: Discovery that Mark may be alive

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - MISSION CONTROL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Vincent and Teddy convince a stunned Annie that Mark is alive by showing evidence: cleaned solar panels, moved rover, and dismantled MDV components.
scenes.turningPointNote: NASA leadership confirms Mark is alive

scenes.heading: EXT. SPACE - MARS - TO ESTABLISH
scenes.summary: Establishing shot of Mars from space as Mark's voice indicates his dire situation.

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark complains about Commander Lewis's disco music collection while Vicki Sue Robinson plays, showing his maintained sense of humor despite isolation.

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Mark studies Mars maps and calculates the 3,200 km journey to the Ares 4 landing site at Schiaparelli Crater where the return vehicle waits.

scenes.heading: EXT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark begins modifying Rover 2 by installing the large battery from Rover 1 to extend its range for the long journey.

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Mark explains the three major problems with his plan: limited rover range, 50-day journey in cramped conditions, and lack of NASA contact.

scenes.heading: EXT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark successfully attaches the spare battery to Rover 2 using a makeshift harness system.

scenes.heading: EXT. HAB - DUSK
scenes.summary: Mark test drives his modified rover with the battery and solar cell setup, finding it functional despite being unwieldy.

scenes.heading: INT. ROVER - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Inside the freezing rover at night, Mark realizes he faces a deadly choice between using battery power for heat or freezing to death.

scenes.heading: EXT. MARS - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark decides to solve his heating problem by digging up the dangerous Radioisotope Thermoelectric Generator despite safety protocols.

scenes.heading: INT. ROVER - DAY
scenes.summary: Now warm from the radioactive heat source, Mark drives while sweating and listening to more disco music, showing his practical problem-solving.

scenes.heading: EXT. MARS - DAY
scenes.summary: The rover travels across the Martian landscape toward the Hab while disco music continues playing.

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - PRESS ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: NASA holds press conference about Mark's survival, with Vincent explaining Mark's rover tests and potential journey to the Ares 4 site.

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - DAY
scenes.summary: Annie coaches Vincent on media relations while walking through NASA, emphasizing the need to avoid reminding people Mark might die.

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - CONFERENCE ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: NASA department heads meet to discuss Mark's 76-kilometer journey, with Mitch Henderson questioning the mission parameters and timeline.

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Mark's Hab has become a thriving greenhouse with 400 potato plants, providing sustainable food, but he still needs to solve communication with NASA.

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - NIGHT
scenes.summary: While studying maps, Mark has a breakthrough realization about solving his communication problem, focusing on Chryse Planitia location.
scenes.turningPointNote: Mark discovers solution to communication problem

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - MISSION CONTROL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: NASA tracks Mark's unexpected course change, with Vincent and Mindy puzzled by his route until Vincent realizes the destination.

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - BREAKROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Vincent uses a Mars poster to plot Mark's course and realizes Mark is heading to an old NASA landing site, solving the mystery.

scenes.heading: EXT. MARS - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark's rover approaches his destination where something metallic glints in the sand, buried and waiting to be excavated.

scenes.heading: INT. JPL LOBBY - DAY
scenes.summary: Vincent arrives at JPL in Pasadena to meet with Bruce about Mark's plan to resurrect old NASA equipment.

scenes.heading: INT/EXT. JPL STORAGE - DAY
scenes.summary: Bruce explains the 1997 mission failure to Vincent while they head to meet the original team, noting the equipment exceeded expectations.

scenes.heading: EXT. MARS - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark continues digging at the site, gradually revealing more of the buried metallic NASA equipment.

scenes.heading: INT. JPL - GARAGE
scenes.summary: Vincent sees the Pathfinder replica at JPL - the 1996 spacecraft with its lander and Sojourner rover components.

scenes.heading: EXT. MARS - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark successfully uncovers Pathfinder and transports the lander back to his Hab using Rover 2 on Sol 109.

scenes.heading: EXT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark performs delicate surgery on Pathfinder, replacing its failed battery with an environment heater to restore power.

scenes.heading: INT. JPL GARAGE - DAY
scenes.summary: Pathfinder's boot sequence initiates successfully, running diagnostics and beginning to search for Earth's telemetry signal.

scenes.heading: INT. JPL GARAGE - DAY
scenes.summary: The JPL team watches as Pathfinder's status appears on their screens, confirming Mark has successfully reactivated the spacecraft.

scenes.heading: EXT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark watches Pathfinder's antenna orient toward Earth and begins dancing in celebration of reestablishing communication.
scenes.turningPointNote: Communication with Earth restored

scenes.heading: INT. JPL GARAGE - DAY
scenes.summary: JPL's Tim Grimes confirms receiving Pathfinder's signal and prepares to download the first panoramic image from Mars.

scenes.heading: EXT. MARS - DAY
scenes.summary: The Pathfinder camera focuses on Mark's message card reading 'YES' confirming his successful communication establishment.

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark explains his plan to create a hexadecimal communication system using camera angles since the regular alphabet won't work.

scenes.heading: EXT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark sets up cards marked A-F and 0-9 in a circle around Pathfinder's camera to create his hexadecimal communication array.

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark finds an ASCII table on Johanssen's laptop, noting her collection of vintage computer games with affectionate humor.

scenes.heading: INT. JPL GARAGE - DAY
scenes.summary: At JPL, Tim uses the ASCII chart to operate Pathfinder's camera for the new communication system.

scenes.heading: EXT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Pathfinder's camera moves between Mark's hexadecimal cards as the communication system becomes operational.

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark decodes NASA's first message asking 'HOW ALIVE' and begins writing his response explaining his survival.

scenes.heading: INT. JPL GARAGE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: NASA receives Mark's explanation about being impaled by the antenna and the crew's reasonable assumption of his death.

scenes.heading: INT. ROVER - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Mark hacks the rover's operating system per NASA's instructions, linking it to Pathfinder for direct text communication with Vincent.
scenes.turningPointNote: Direct communication established

scenes.heading: INT. JPL - GARAGE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Vincent dictates messages to Mark through the new communication system, coordinating with Bruce and Tim at JPL.

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - MISSION CONTROL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: NASA's leadership watches as Vincent's message of worldwide support and rescue plans is transmitted to Mark.

scenes.heading: INT. ROVER - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Mark reads NASA's message about supply missions and rescue plans, his first real human contact since the storm.

scenes.heading: INT. JPL GARAGE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Mark responds positively about not dying and asks about his crew, prompting an uncomfortable moment for Vincent and Bruce.

scenes.heading: INT. ROVER - NIGHT
scenes.summary: NASA reveals they haven't told the crew Mark is alive yet, needing them to focus on their mission, creating emotional tension.
scenes.turningPointNote: Revelation that crew doesn't know Mark is alive

scenes.heading: INT. JPL GARAGE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Tim reads Mark's profanity-laden response at JPL, with Vincent asking Mark to watch his language since everything is broadcast live worldwide

scenes.heading: INT. ROVER - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Mark in the rover reads Vincent's warning about live broadcasts and types another response with narrowed eyes

scenes.heading: INT. JPL GARAGE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The JPL team reads Mark's second response and goes pale, with Vincent hanging his head while Tim tries not to smile

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - TEDDY’S OFFICE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Teddy explains to Mitch that he had to tell the President what a 'bureaucratic felcher' is, while Mitch argues they need to tell the Hermes crew about Mark

scenes.heading: EXT. HERMES - PRESENT - SPACE
scenes.summary: The Hermes spacecraft continues its journey to Earth four months after Mars departure, with Commander Lewis staring out at space

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - FLIGHT DECK - SPACE
scenes.summary: Lewis alone on the flight deck receives communication from Johanssen about an incoming data dump

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - CORRIDOR - SPACE
scenes.summary: Lewis and Martinez float through the Hermes corridors, with Martinez eager to see photos of his three-year-old son's birthday party

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - REC ROOM - SPACE
scenes.summary: The crew gathers for the data dump in the rec room, receiving personal messages and discovering a voice message addressed to the entire crew
scenes.turningPointNote: Setup for crew learning Mark is alive

scenes.heading: EXT. MARS - SPACE - TO ESTABLISH
scenes.summary: Establishing shot of Mars on Sol 128

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark waters his thriving potato plants while noting that NASA won't stop talking to him now

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark talks to camera about NASA micromanaging his crops, proudly showing his lush potato plants and joking about being the best botanist on the planet

scenes.heading: EXT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark poses outside in his suit for a photo, holding up a notecard we cannot see

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - CONFERENCE ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Annie reviews Mark's photo showing him as the Fonz saying 'Ayyyyyyy!' while the team discusses the rescue mission and supply launch plans

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DUSK
scenes.summary: The HAB explosively depressurizes in slow motion, destroying all of Mark's crops in the process
scenes.turningPointNote: ★KEY MOMENT★ Catastrophic failure destroys Mark's food supply

scenes.heading: INT/EXT. AIRLOCK - DUSK
scenes.summary: The airlock crashes violently down a hillside with Mark inside, his faceplate shattering as he's thrown around like a ragdoll

scenes.heading: INT. AIRLOCK - DUSK
scenes.summary: Mark regains consciousness in the damaged airlock, bleeding and discovering the HAB is destroyed while frantically searching for an air leak that threatens his survival

scenes.heading: INT/EXT. AIRLOCK/HAB - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Mark navigates back to the collapsed HAB using a patched suit with fabric covering his broken faceplate, using his arm computer's camera to see

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Mark surveys the devastated HAB interior and his completely ruined potato crops - his lifeline destroyed - then quietly begins cleaning up the mess

scenes.heading: INT. ROVER - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Mark sits in the rover composing how to explain the disaster to NASA

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - CONFERENCE ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: NASA team learns of the complete crop failure - Mark now has only 200 sols of existing potatoes plus rations, totaling just 400 sols when he needs 1400
scenes.turningPointNote: Mark's timeline becomes impossible - needs rescue acceleration

scenes.heading: INT. JPL BULLPEN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Bruce and his JPL team receive the impossible timeline challenge, with Bruce saying they'll all need a change of clothes

scenes.heading: EXT. HERMES - SPACE
scenes.summary: The Hermes continues toward Earth on Sol 136 as Martinez begins composing a message

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES REC ROOM - SPACE
scenes.summary: Martinez types a humorous message to Mark apologizing for leaving him behind and making jokes about the extra room on the Hermes

scenes.heading: INT. ROVER - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Mark reads Martinez's message and responds with jokes about blowing up the HAB but Lewis's disco music surviving, and about Mars's vast horizons

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES REC ROOM - SPACE
scenes.summary: Martinez reads Mark's response about the cramped Hermes versus Mars's vast horizons and promises to pass along Mark's greetings

scenes.heading: EXT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark returns to work clearing debris from the destroyed HAB and examining the hole in the airlock

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark patches the airlock hole with canvas and duct tape, successfully repressurizing the HAB as the makeshift patch holds

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Mark inventories his remaining potatoes while wind batters the canvas patch, trying not to think about his life depending on duct tape

scenes.heading: INT. JPL - RICH’S OFFICE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Rich Purnell sleeps in his JPL office as orbital computations run on his computer, awakened by Mike asking about probe courses for the four-hundred-four day mission

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - MISSION CONTROL - DAY
scenes.summary: Vincent dictates to Mark about the Iris probe taking 414 days to reach him with enough food to last until Ares 4

scenes.heading: INT. ROVER - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark reads the message about the Iris probe being named after the Greek goddess of rainbows who traveled the heavens

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - MISSION CONTROL - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark responds to NASA's message about the Iris probe with 'Gay probe coming to save me. Got it.'

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - CONFERENCE ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: NASA team discusses the extremely expensive Iris probe timeline, with Bruce needing fifteen more days while Teddy pushes to reduce testing and inspections

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark explains his severe calorie restriction on Sol 154, eating one ration every three days supplemented with potatoes, looking notably depressed

scenes.heading: EXT. CAPE CANAVERAL - DAY
scenes.summary: The Iris probe is mounted and readied for launch at Cape Canaveral with final supplies being loaded

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - MISSION CONTROL - DAY
scenes.summary: Mission Control prepares for launch with Mitch as Flight Director beginning the Launch Status Check, invoking multiple gods for help

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - MISSION CONTROL - DAY
scenes.summary: Teddy watches from observation with a prepared success speech as the final countdown begins

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - MISSION CONTROL - DAY
scenes.summary: Annie paces nervously in her office watching nine televisions as the countdown continues

scenes.heading: INT. JPL - CONTROL ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Bruce and his JPL engineers watch in rapt attention as the countdown reaches its final seconds

scenes.heading: INT. JPL - RICH’S OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: Rich Purnell works obliviously on orbital calculations, ignoring the launch entirely

scenes.heading: EXT. CAPE CANAVERAL LAUNCHPAD - DAY
scenes.summary: The Iris probe lifts off from Cape Canaveral as the countdown reaches zero

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - MISSION CONTROL - DAY
scenes.summary: Mission Control celebrates initially, but the Launcher Interface reports a concerning shimmy in the rocket

scenes.heading: INT. HULL - DAY
scenes.summary: Inside the hull, violent shaking causes a critical bolt to crack under the acceleration forces

scenes.heading: EXT. IRIS CRAFT - DAY
scenes.summary: The first stage jettison goes wrong, with the spent stage catching and swinging unnaturally as the second stage ignites

scenes.heading: INT. HULL - DAY
scenes.summary: The acceleration shears the bolt completely, causing the Iris payload to break free from its supports and slam into the hull

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - CONTROL ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Mission Control erupts in alarms as the probe spins wildly and they lose all signal contact with the tumbling spacecraft
scenes.turningPointNote: ★KEY MOMENT★ Iris probe completely destroyed - Mark's rescue mission fails catastrophically

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - VINCENT’S OFFICE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Vincent sits alone in his dark office at night when Mark's message arrives asking how the launch went

scenes.heading: EXT. SPACE - TO ESTABLISH
scenes.summary: Mars appears against the darkness of space on Sol 186

scenes.heading: EXT. MARS - DAWN
scenes.summary: Mark stands on Mars at dawn, recording a message to Commander Lewis about checking on his parents if he dies, saying he's dying for something greater than himself

scenes.heading: EXT. CNSA - TO ESTABLISH - DAY
scenes.summary: Establishing shot of the China National Space Administration headquarters in Beijing
scenes.turningPointNote: Introduction of potential new solution through China

scenes.heading: INT. CNSA - DAY
scenes.summary: News conference shows Teddy and Vincent explaining the protein cube failure that caused launch problems, with reporters questioning the decision to skip inspections to meet the launch window.

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - TEDDY’S OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: Teddy receives a phone call with terms that bring him relief, closing his eyes as hope washes over him before agreeing to the proposal.
scenes.turningPointNote: China's offer to help provides critical breakthrough in rescue efforts

scenes.heading: INT. JPL - BULLPEN - DAY
scenes.summary: Bruce energetically addresses his team about building a new Iris probe in 28 days thanks to China's help, having previously completed one in 63 days.

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - SUPER COMPUTER ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Rich Purnell discovers something extraordinary in his calculations, running the numbers again before grinning at the impossible solution on his screen.

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - HALLWAYS - DAY
scenes.summary: Rich hurries through NASA hallways with urgent purpose.

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - VINCENT’S OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: Rich interrupts Vincent's phone call, insisting he hang up immediately and identifying himself as an astrodynamics expert with critical information.

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - CONFERENCE ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Secret meeting dubbed 'Project Elrond' where Rich reveals his plan to get Hermes back to Mars, using office supplies to demonstrate his revolutionary maneuver to the stunned NASA leadership.
scenes.turningPointNote: Rich Purnell Maneuver is revealed as potential game-changing rescue plan

scenes.heading: EXT. HAB - DAY/DUSK
scenes.summary: Mark reaches his breaking point, unable to continue his routine maintenance, sitting on a hill as his suit alarms blare in the cold until he spots something metallic in the distance.
scenes.turningPointNote: Mark's emotional collapse followed by discovery of metallic object marks crucial psychological and plot turning point

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - MISSION CONTROL - DAY
scenes.summary: NASA observes Mark conducting systematic EVAs in 300-meter intervals toward the rover, receiving a jumbled transmission of numbers and data.

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Mark addresses the camera while processing rock samples, solemnly working through the abandoned research schedules of his crewmates with determination.

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - FLIGHT DECK - SPACE
scenes.summary: Vogel receives a strange email from his wife titled 'Our Children' with an unreadable attachment that concerns him.

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES GYM - SPACE
scenes.summary: Vogel approaches Johannsen in the Hermes gym seeking help with his mysterious email problem.

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES GYM - SPACE
scenes.summary: Johannsen discovers the attachment contains math equations and course maneuvers for Hermes, specifically the 'Rich Purnell Maneuver' referencing Mars.

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES REC ROOM - SPACE
scenes.summary: Lewis briefs the Hermes crew on the Rich Purnell Maneuver, explaining NASA rejected the plan and discussing whether to defy orders and force NASA's hand.
scenes.turningPointNote: Hermes crew faces critical decision to mutiny against NASA orders

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - MISSION CONTROL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Night shift Mission Control receives cryptic message calling Rich Purnell a 'steely-eyed missile man' followed by alarms as Hermes deliberately changes course.
scenes.turningPointNote: Hermes crew executes the Rich Purnell Maneuver, forcing NASA's hand

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - TEDDY’S OFFICE - DAWN
scenes.summary: Teddy confronts Mitch about the crew's mutiny, discussing the political and personal costs while announcing NASA will publicly support the crew's decision.

scenes.heading: EXT. MARS
scenes.summary: Title card shows Sol 219 as sunlight creeps across Schiaparelli Crater with sound of drilling, establishing the new phase of Mark's survival.

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark explains the plan to reach the pre-positioned Ares-4 MAV at Schiaparelli Crater 3,200 kilometers away, grinning at the prospect of being caught in space.

scenes.heading: EXT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark performs grueling work drilling hundreds of holes through Rover Two's roof, then using primitive tools to break through the metal.

scenes.heading: EXT. MARS - DAY - BEGIN MONTAGE
scenes.summary: Montage begins with Mark positioning his partially-disassembled rover for NASA to photograph and analyze his modifications.

scenes.heading: INT. NASA ROVER ROOM - DAY - MONTAGE
scenes.summary: NASA engineers study Mark's photographs and begin sketching solutions for his rover modifications.

scenes.heading: EXT. CNSA - DAY - MONTAGE
scenes.summary: Teddy and Mitch meet with Chinese space officials at CNSA to formalize their cooperation agreement.

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - KITCHEN - DAY - MONTAGE
scenes.summary: Mark inventories his remaining food supplies, labeling special rations for 'Departure,' 'Birthday,' and 'Last Meal' while eating potatoes.

scenes.heading: EXT. HERMES - SPACE - MONTAGE
scenes.summary: The Hermes approaches Earth, slowing its rotation as part of the return trajectory.

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - VIDEO BOOTH - REC ROOM - SPACE - MONTAGE
scenes.summary: Martinez has an emotional video call with his upset wife about extending the mission 533 more days, ultimately reconciling through the screen.

scenes.heading: INT. JPL - WHITE ROOM - DAY - MONTAGE
scenes.summary: Bruce oversees the Iris 2 Probe being loaded into shipping containers while Chinese CNSA team members observe the process.

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - VIDEO BOOTH - REC ROOM - SPACE - MONTAGE
scenes.summary: Lewis has a loving video call with her husband who shows her a vintage ABBA album he found, bringing her joy.

scenes.heading: EXT. LAUNCH PAD - CHINA - DAY - MONTAGE
scenes.summary: The Iris 2 Probe is attached to its booster rocket at the Chinese launch pad.

scenes.heading: INT. CNSA - DAY - MONTAGE
scenes.summary: Mitch argues heatedly with the Chinese Flight Director over protocols, with Teddy watching the cultural clash unfold.

scenes.heading: EXT. HAB - DAY - MONTAGE
scenes.summary: Mark seals and pressurizes his tent modification on Rover 2, testing it like a hot-air balloon for leaks.

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - WORK ROOM - MONTAGE
scenes.summary: Vincent's engineering team mirrors Mark's work, trying to figure out how to fit the Oxygenator into the pop tent design.

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES FLIGHT DECK - SPACE - MONTAGE
scenes.summary: Vogel entertains his wife and children via video call, performing low-gravity tricks that make his kids laugh with delight.

scenes.heading: INT. CNSA - DAY - MONTAGE
scenes.summary: The Chinese rocket launches successfully with the Taiyang Shen, leading to celebrations and handshakes between American and Chinese teams.
scenes.turningPointNote: Successful launch of Chinese rocket marks critical milestone in rescue mission

scenes.heading: EXT. MARS - DAY - MONTAGE
scenes.summary: Mark drives his modified Rover 2 across Martian dunes, bobbing his head to music in good spirits.

scenes.heading: EXT. IRIS 2 PROBE - DAY (STOCK)
scenes.summary: The Iris 2 probe successfully separates from its booster rocket in space.

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES FLIGHT DECK - MONTAGE
scenes.summary: Martinez takes control of the probe from Hermes, piloting it toward the ship for docking procedures.

scenes.heading: EXT. HERMES - SPACE - MONTAGE
scenes.summary: The probe approaches Hermes as the critical docking procedure begins in space.

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - AIRLOCK 3 - SPACE - MONTAGE
scenes.summary: Beck in his EVA suit guides the probe to the docking port while tethered to Hermes.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT HERMES - REC ROOM - SPACE - MONTAGE
scenes.summary: Johannsen watches anxiously through a window as Beck works to secure the docking procedure.

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - AIRLOCK 3 - SPACE - MONTAGE
scenes.summary: Beck continues guiding the probe to successful docking while secured in his EVA suit.

scenes.heading: EXT. HERMES - SPACE - END MONTAGE
scenes.summary: Hermes rotates through space on its return journey to Mars, leaving Earth behind as the montage ends.
scenes.turningPointNote: Successful supply mission completion sets stage for final rescue phase

scenes.heading: EXT. MARS
scenes.summary: Title card shows Sol 461 on Mars over six months later as Mark contemplates laws and jurisdiction on Mars.

scenes.heading: EXT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: The transformed rover setup resembles a gypsy caravan of NASA equipment as Mark explains international maritime law applying to Mars.

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: The Hab interior is stripped and modified, showing the extensive changes made over six months of survival.

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Gaunt, bearded Mark declares himself a space pirate for commandeering the Ares 4 lander without permission, finding it better than winning a Nobel Prize.

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark's final day in the Hab: shaving his beard, organizing experiment boxes, and powering down all systems into darkness and silence.

scenes.heading: EXT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark depressurizes the Hab and stares at it with gratitude for keeping him alive through his ordeal.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROVER - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark loads his remaining supplies including frozen potatoes and a ration labeled 'Goodbye, Mars' into the crowded Rover 2.

scenes.heading: INT. ROVER - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark powers up Rover 1's systems, preparing for the journey to Schiaparelli Crater.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROVER - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark begins his final journey across Mars, driving away from the Hab site toward the horizon and his potential rescue.
scenes.turningPointNote: Mark begins the final phase of his survival journey, leaving the Hab forever

scenes.heading: EXT. MARS - VARIOUS - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark reflects on being the first human to explore various locations on Mars as he leaves footprints in the red dirt, noting Phobos in the sky and contemplating his unique isolation on an entire planet.

scenes.heading: EXT. MARS - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark sits outside the rover in his EVA suit waiting for solar panels to charge, taking in the beautiful Martian landscape with Phobos crossing the sky.

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - MISSION CONTROL - DAY
scenes.summary: At NASA Mission Control, Vincent checks on Mark's progress with Mindy, who reports he's following schedule and maintaining good morale, having asked to be called 'Captain Blondebeard.'

scenes.heading: EXT. MARS - DAY
scenes.summary: The rover crosses Mars in quiet solitude on Sol 494, moving at maximum speed of 25 kph - the only thing moving on the entire planet.

scenes.heading: EXT. MARS - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark watches the Martian horizon from the rover.

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - VINCENT’S OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: Bruce and Mitch present Vincent with plans requiring the MAV to be 5,000 kilograms lighter for Mark to achieve intercept velocity with Hermes, demonstrating with a scale model.
scenes.turningPointNote: Critical technical challenge introduced that will determine rescue feasibility

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - MISSION CONTROL - DAY
scenes.summary: Mindy reads Mark's profanity-laden response to the weight reduction plan while Vincent hopes Mark meant it in an excited rather than angry way.

scenes.heading: INT. ROVER - ARES 4 MAV SITE - DAY
scenes.summary: On Sol 538 at the Ares 4 MAV site, Mark expresses his distress about NASA's plan to launch him in what he describes as a convertible with no controls.

scenes.heading: EXT. MAV - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark stands at the base of the MAV holding a large wrench like a weapon, preparing to modify the vehicle as ABBA's 'Waterloo' begins to play.

scenes.heading: INT. MAV - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark tears out the acceleration chairs from the MAV cockpit.

scenes.heading: EXT. MAV - DAY
scenes.summary: The acceleration chairs hit the dirt outside the MAV in a growing pile.

scenes.heading: INT. MAV - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark continues dismantling the MAV by tearing out control panels, enjoying the destructive work.

scenes.heading: EXT. MAV - DAY
scenes.summary: Control panels join the growing pile of discarded MAV components as 'Waterloo' plays.

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES FLIGHT DECK - SPACE
scenes.summary: On Hermes, Martinez struggles with flight simulations, repeatedly crashing and killing the theoretical Mark while Lewis demands he try again.

scenes.heading: INT. MAV COCKPIT - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark waits in the MAV airlock surrounded by stripped equipment.

scenes.heading: EXT. MAV - DAY
scenes.summary: The exterior of the MAV now resembles a junkyard as Mark removes hull panels.

scenes.heading: EXT. MAV - DAY
scenes.summary: The nose airlock breaks free and tumbles toward the camera, blacking out the frame.

scenes.heading: EXT. MAV - DUSK
scenes.summary: At dusk, Mark sits exhausted surveying his transformed MAV - the front torn off and covered with hab canvas, equipment scattered everywhere like a Martian junkyard.
scenes.turningPointNote: MAV modification complete, point of no return for rescue attempt

scenes.heading: EXT. HERMES - SPACE
scenes.summary: The Hermes halts rotation as it approaches Mars on Sol 560, beginning the rescue phase.

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES REC ROOM - SPACE
scenes.summary: Lewis briefs the crew on rescue roles: Martinez flying MAV, Johanssen on ascent systems, Beck and Vogel in airlock for intercept with Beck handling Mark's medical needs and using tethers for the grab.
scenes.turningPointNote: Final rescue plan established, crew roles assigned for critical mission

scenes.heading: INT. POP UP TENT - DAY
scenes.summary: On Sol 561, Mark eats his final ration pack labeled 'Goodbye, Mars' in silence inside his makeshift pressurized tent.

scenes.heading: EXT. NASA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Global media crews create chaos outside NASA as networks worldwide cover the rescue attempt as the biggest story of the century.

scenes.heading: EXT. TIMES SQUARE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: New Year's Eve-level crowds gather in Times Square watching Jumbotron coverage of the Watney rescue.

scenes.heading: INT. MISSION CONTROL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: In Mission Control, Vincent explains to Annie and Teddy that they can't help due to 24-minute communication delay, while the 12-minute launch happens in real-time.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREETS - BEIJING - DAY
scenes.summary: Crowds in Beijing watch screens as Johanssen reports perfect engine alignment over communications.

scenes.heading: EXT. ST. PETERSBERG - DAY
scenes.summary: Cold crowds in St. Petersburg's Palace Square watch screens as Johanssen reports communications are optimal.

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - FLIGHT DECK - SPACE
scenes.summary: Lewis keys the console and tells Mark he has about two minutes until launch, asking how he's doing.

scenes.heading: INT. MAV - COCKPIT - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark struggles to control his emotions in the MAV cockpit, his voice breaking as he thanks Lewis for coming back for him.

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES FLIGHT DECK - SPACE
scenes.summary: Lewis warns Mark about heavy G-forces and tells him it's okay to pass out, while Mark jokes about no barrel-rolls as the crew confirms their stations.

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - AIRLOCK 2 - SPACE
scenes.summary: Beck floats in the open airlock with Mars blazing below as Vogel confirms secondary recovery status, both ready for their roles.

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - FLIGHT DECK - SPACE
scenes.summary: Lewis calls for pilot confirmation as the final pre-launch check.

scenes.heading: INT. MAV - COCKPIT - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark, strapped into his acceleration seat in his EVA suit, gives final 'Go' confirmation for launch.
scenes.turningPointNote: Final launch confirmation given, point of no return reached

scenes.heading: EXT. VARIOUS - DAY/NIGHT
scenes.summary: Crowds around the world erupt in cheers as they hear Mark's voice confirming he's ready for launch.

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - MISSION CONTROL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Lewis announces to Mission Control that Hermes is go for launch and will proceed on schedule with 10 seconds to launch mark.

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - FLIGHT DECK - SPACE
scenes.summary: Martinez starts main engines as Johanssen counts down and Lewis warns Mark to hang on with about five seconds remaining.

scenes.heading: INT. MAV - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark tells Lewis he'll see her soon as the final countdown reaches zero and liftoff slams him back into his acceleration couch.
scenes.turningPointNote: Launch sequence initiated, Mark's rescue attempt begins

scenes.heading: EXT. LAUNCHSITE - DAY
scenes.summary: The MAV launches with unprecedented force, accelerating faster than any manned ship in space travel history.

scenes.heading: INT. MAV - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark struggles to remain conscious as the ship shakes violently and he watches the hab canvas patch begin to rip from the acceleration forces.

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - FLIGHT DECK - SPACE
scenes.summary: Johanssen reports Mark is too low on altitude and velocity while Lewis tries to contact an unresponsive Mark as Martinez fights the controls.

scenes.heading: INT. MAV - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark loses consciousness as the canvas rips free, revealing Mars' red atmosphere thinning to black space and stars as he rockets toward the heavens.

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - FLIGHT DECK - SPACE
scenes.summary: After main engine shutdown, Johanssen reports Mark is well below target with 11 m/s intercept velocity and 68 kilometers distance, while Beck explains Mark likely passed out from 12 G's.
scenes.turningPointNote: Critical problem revealed - Mark is far off target trajectory

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - AIRLOCK 2 - SPACE
scenes.summary: Beck expresses shock at the 68-kilometer intercept distance in the airlock.

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - FLIGHT DECK - SPACE
scenes.summary: Lewis commands the crew to work the problem as they discover the MAV has no remaining fuel and they must use Hermes attitude thrusters to close the gap, requiring fuel needed for the trip home.

scenes.heading: EXT. HERMES - SPACE
scenes.summary: The Hermes fires its attitude thrusters and changes course toward Mark's position.

scenes.heading: INT. MAV - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark regains consciousness in pain, sees Mars' breathtaking horizon below, gives the planet his middle finger, and contacts Hermes reporting broken ribs and that the nose cone ripped off early in ascent.

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - FLIGHT DECK - SPACE
scenes.summary: Lewis confirms they corrected intercept range but reveals the critical problem of 42 meters per second intercept velocity to Mark.

scenes.heading: INT. MAV - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark processes the devastating news about the intercept velocity problem with a simple 'Well. Shit.'

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - FLIGHT DECK - SPACE
scenes.summary: As everyone struggles to find a solution, Mark interrupts the silence to contact Lewis.

scenes.heading: INT. MAV - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark proposes puncturing his EVA suit glove to use escaping air as a thruster to fly to Hermes, believing he could direct it since the source would be on his arm.
scenes.turningPointNote: Mark proposes desperate improvised solution to save himself

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - FLIGHT DECK - SPACE
scenes.summary: Lewis points out Mark would have no control and would be eyeballing the intercept with barely controllable thrust, but Mark begins to counter with 'Consider this.'

scenes.heading: INT. MAV - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark jokes about getting to fly around like Iron Man using his punctured suit plan

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - FLIGHT DECK - SPACE
scenes.summary: Lewis considers Mark's Iron Man plan, then pivots to using atmospheric thrust by blowing the VAL airlock
scenes.turningPointNote: Lewis shifts from rejecting Mark's plan to developing a new explosive solution

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - FLIGHT DECK - SPACE
scenes.summary: Martinez questions how to remotely open airlock doors, Lewis realizes they need Vogel to make a bomb

scenes.heading: INT. MAV - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark expresses disappointment at missing out on the bomb-making activity

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - AIRLOCK 2 - SPACE
scenes.summary: Vogel reluctantly agrees to make a bomb despite acknowledging it's a terrible idea for a spacecraft

scenes.heading: INT. MISSION CONTROL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Mission Control erupts in chaos when Lewis announces their plan to deliberately breach the VAL

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - FLIGHT DECK - SPACE
scenes.summary: Lewis orders Beck to place the explosive charge on the inner airlock door

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - AIRLOCK 2 - SPACE
scenes.summary: Beck acknowledges orders to climb along the hull to Airlock 2 after placing the bomb

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES/MAV - SPACE - INTERCUT
scenes.summary: Mark prepares to puncture his suit while Lewis asserts her command authority and refuses to call off the mission

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES REC ROOM - SPACE
scenes.summary: Vogel creates a sugar and liquid oxygen bomb while explaining its explosive power to Johanssen

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - VAL (AIRLOCK 1) - SPACE
scenes.summary: Beck and Johanssen share an intimate moment as she gives him the bomb and kisses his faceplate

scenes.heading: INT/EXT. HERMES - VAL (AIRLOCK 1) - SPACE
scenes.summary: Beck secures the improvised bomb to the inner airlock door as space is revealed

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - FLIGHT DECK - SPACE
scenes.summary: Martinez calculates they'll still miss the intercept by 260 meters even with optimal VAL explosion

scenes.heading: EXT. HERMES - SPACE
scenes.summary: Beck climbs along Hermes' hull using handholds to reach Airlock 2

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - AIRLOCK 2 - SPACE
scenes.summary: Lewis waits fully suited in Airlock 2, announcing she'll go untethered due to intercept distance
scenes.turningPointNote: Lewis commits to the most dangerous part of the rescue personally

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - FLIGHT DECK - SPACE
scenes.summary: The crew braces for deceleration as Johanssen counts down to bomb detonation

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - VAL (AIRLOCK 1) - SPACE
scenes.summary: The bomb explodes, ripping the airlock door apart and slamming Lewis and Beck against the wall

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - FLIGHT DECK - SPACE
scenes.summary: The crew endures deceleration as the ship stabilizes and they assess damage

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - AIRLOCK 2 - SPACE
scenes.summary: Lewis launches herself from Airlock 2 toward Mark with 12 meters per second relative velocity
scenes.turningPointNote: Lewis commits to the final leap into space

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - FLIGHT DECK - SPACE
scenes.summary: Johanssen calculates their intercept range has worsened to 312 meters

scenes.heading: INT. MAV - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark realizes the bad news means he'll wave as he passes by, while Lewis spots the MAV

scenes.heading: EXT. HERMES - SPACE
scenes.summary: Lewis sails through space using her MMU, seeing the distant rotating MAV and realizing she won't make it

scenes.heading: INT. MAV - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark steels himself, unclips his harness, and punctures his suit to execute the Iron Man plan
scenes.turningPointNote: Mark commits to his desperate gambit by puncturing his suit

scenes.heading: EXT. MAV - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark soars out of the tumbling MAV like Iron Man, having the time of his life with visual on Lewis

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - FLIGHT DECK - SPACE
scenes.summary: The crew monitors relative velocities as Mark and Lewis rocket toward each other

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - AIRLOCK 2 - SPACE
scenes.summary: Beck watches from Airlock 2 as the two figures approach with 24 meters distance remaining

scenes.heading: EXT. HERMES - SPACE
scenes.summary: Lewis counter-thrusts with her MMU to slow down as Mark approaches at 11 meters distance

scenes.heading: EXT. SPACE
scenes.summary: Mark cuts through space with Mars behind him as the two astronauts reach for each other

scenes.heading: EXT. SPACE - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark and Lewis make contact and float together, Mark's first human contact in ages
scenes.turningPointNote: The successful rescue contact after 500+ days of isolation

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - MISSION CONTROL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Mission Control erupts in cheers when Lewis announces she got him

scenes.heading: EXT. ST. PETERSBERG - DAY
scenes.summary: Celebrations explode across Russia as news of the rescue spreads

scenes.heading: EXT. TIMES SQUARE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Times Square and New York celebrate as news reports break the rescue story

scenes.heading: EXT. BEIJING - DAY
scenes.summary: Celebrations continue in Beijing and China

scenes.heading: EXT./INT. AIRLOCK - DAY
scenes.summary: Lewis guides Mark to the airlock where the crew embraces him after his collapse from exhaustion
scenes.turningPointNote: Mark's reunion with his full crew after isolation

scenes.heading: EXT. EARTH - TO ESTABLISH
scenes.summary: Earth appears warm and welcoming from space - home at last

scenes.heading: EXT. PARK - JOHNSON SPACE CENTER - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark sits alone on a bench at Johnson Space Center, basking in a beautiful day on Day 1

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - TEACHING THEATER - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark teaches NASA's Astronaut Candidate Program, joking about surviving by farming in his own waste

scenes.heading: EXT. LAUNCHPAD - DAY
scenes.summary: A new spacecraft is readied for launch as Annie begins mission briefing procedures

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - PRESS ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Annie and Vincent brief the press on the Ares 5 mission's 414-day journey to Mars

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - MISSION CONTROL
scenes.summary: Mission Control prepares for launch with Mindy as CAPCOM and Brendan as Flight Director

scenes.heading: EXT. JPL - MORNING
scenes.summary: Bruce Ng and his JPL team enjoy breakfast while watching Ares 5 launch coverage

scenes.heading: INT. SUBURBAN HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Alex Vogel plays with his five blonde children at home during the launch coverage

scenes.heading: INT. SPACECRAFT - DAY
scenes.summary: Ares 5 astronaut Wen Jiang, the first Chinese national to Mars, runs pre-flight checks

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - MISSION CONTROL
scenes.summary: Chinese officials Zhu Tao and Guo Ming watch proudly from Mission Control

scenes.heading: EXT. PARK - MORNING
scenes.summary: Retired Mitch Henderson watches his grandson play at the park

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL - DAY
scenes.summary: Chris Beck visits his wife Beth Johanssen and their newborn baby girl in the hospital

scenes.heading: INT. SPACECRAFT - DAY
scenes.summary: Rick Martinez grins with adrenaline as the launch countdown reaches final seconds

scenes.heading: INT. HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Melissa Lewis watches the launch from home surrounded by disco memorabilia and her medals

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - MISSION CONTROL
scenes.summary: Mission Control erupts in cheers as the Ares 5 spacecraft launches successfully
scenes.turningPointNote: The successful launch of humanity's return to Mars

scenes.heading: EXT. SPACE
scenes.summary: Earth appears blue and beautiful from space as the story ends with fade out
